sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.88 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: We define the length\/$l(g)$of an Engel element$g$of a group$G$to be the smallest number$l$such that$[h,g;\, l]=1$for all$h G$. Here$[h,g;\, 1]=[h,g]$and$[h,g;\, i + 1]=[[h,g;\, i],g]$. Does there exist a polynomial function$(x,y)$such that$l(uv) (l(u),l(v))$? Up to now, it is unknown whether a product of Engel elements is again an Engel element. { V.\,A.\,Roman'kov} No, it does not (L.\,V.\,Dolba...
